notes:
An editor-selection & dark-mode polish release.

**Fixes & polish**

- **Selecting a tag, link, or block-reference now highlights just that item.**
  Selecting an inline chip used to flash the whole line as selected (while only
  the chip was) and gave no clear cue; it now shows a crisp ring around the chip
  and the misleading line-wide highlight is gone.
- **Shift-clicking a link to open it in the right sidebar no longer leaves a
  stray text selection** streaked across the note.
- **Backspace at the start of a sub-bullet outdents in one step.** On a bullet
  with sub-items it used to take two presses (with a confusing in-between state);
  it now matches indent/outdent behavior in a single press.
- **Dark mode reads better.** The text-selection colour is clearer and selected
  text stays legible; faint secondary/metadata text (the search placeholder,
  sidebar labels, calendar, slash menu) now meets contrast standards; the
  update banner's button, delete/"Saved" messages, the warning banner, tab
  badges, block-reference tints and the settings toggle all use theme colours
  instead of washed-out or hard-to-read baked-in ones; and native scrollbars and
  form controls follow the dark theme.

**Under the hood**

- Native selection rendering for inline-atom node-selections is suppressed in
  favour of a CSS ring (`:has(.ProseMirror-selectednode)`), fixing a WKWebView
  quirk where the selection band ballooned to the whole line.
- A new `--color-warning` design token and a `color-scheme` declaration per
  theme; a `tokens.contrast.test.ts` WCAG guardrail asserts key foreground/
  background pairs clear 4.5:1 / 3:1 in both themes so future palette tweaks
  can't silently regress legibility.
- The first-child Backspace-outdent now routes through the same single-step
  transform as Shift-Tab (B5), avoiding ProseMirror's `liftListItem`
  re-entrancy quirk.
